## STACKED BAR 100% chart, KATA style

Render as SVG (or equivalent) on a 960x540 px canvas.
Every position, size, and color below is exact. No adjectives, no improvisation.

### AI metadata (for agent routing)
```json
{
  "code": "CH-PTW-02",
  "ovp_version": "1.0",
  "intent": [
    "part-to-whole",
    "share",
    "change-over-time"
  ],
  "form": "vertical 100% stacked bar",
  "data_required": "ordered categories + 2 to 4 additive segments; totals normalized away",
  "family": "PTW",
  "alternatives": [
    "CH-PTW-01 (absolute stacked)",
    "CH-PTW-06 (waffle, planned)"
  ],
  "see_instead": [
    {
      "when": "the total itself matters",
      "use": "CH-PTW-01"
    }
  ]
}
```

Use when:
- how the MIX shifts across periods or categories
- the key segment's share is the message
- 2 to 4 additive segments

Do NOT use when:
- the total matters (use CH-PTW-01; 100% hides growth and shrinkage)
- more than 4 segments
- single-period share (a sentence or CH-PTW-06 does it better)

### Input data shape
```json
{
  "title": "string, action title stating the share shift with a number",
  "eyebrow": "string caps, rendered only when the DL chrome asks for it",
  "source": "string, rendered bottom-left, always",
  "unit": "percent, implied; share labels carry the % sign",
  "x": "array of ordered category labels",
  "series": "array of {name, values[]}, first series is the KEY segment whose share is labeled"
}
```

### Rules
- all columns equal height; the mix is the message
- the key (first) segment's share labeled above each column with the % sign
- segments identified by swatch + name at the last column, never a legend
- 4 segments maximum
- the title must state the share shift, not the totals

### Never do this
- using 100% stacked when totals shrink (it hides decline)
- legend boxes
- labeling every segment share (label the key segment only)
- more than 4 segments

### QA before delivering
- [ ] columns equal height
- [ ] key segment share labeled with %
- [ ] segment names at last column, no legend
- [ ] title states the share shift
- [ ] source line present bottom-left
